methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 10 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Lab 91, a Seed-stage semiconductor company founded by Anand Chamarthy, operates within the Chips & Semiconductors sector. The broader comp universe includes 3 public peers (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private companies, with notable private comparables including Cerebras, SambaNova Systems, PsiQuantum, Etched, and Tenstorrent.

The valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method due to data constraints. Stage-Progression analysis applies a 2.5x multiplier to Lab 91's $10M+ total raised, treating this as a proxy for Seed-stage entry value. This yields an implied valuation of $25M. The Peer-Median method is not applicable; while 10 same-sector peers exist, the filter for comparable stage and valuation tier returned insufficient datapoints (0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier comps), falling short of the minimum required for reliable median benchmarking without introducing distortion bias.

The triangulated analysis, constrained to the single applicable method, supports a valuation range of $21M to $29M, with a median of $25M. This narrow range reflects reliance on stage-progression mechanics rather than peer validation. Key uncertainties include the absence of tight-peer comparables at Seed stage within the semiconductor sector and the dependence on stage-multiplier assumptions rather than market-based pricing signals. A more robust analysis would require additional comparable transactions or financial performance data from adjacent-stage peers.
